request.question: This American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan starred an English actress who made her film debut in what?

title: Bad Girls (1994 film)
Brokedown Palace (disambiguation)
The Accused (1988 film)
Brokedown Palace
Kate Beckinsale
Immediate Family (film)
Love Field (film)
Over the Edge (film)
Unlawful Entry (film)
Reform School Girl (1994 film)
sentences: Bad Girls is a 1994 American western film directed by Jonathan Kaplan, and written by Ken Friedman and Yolande Turner.
It stars Madeleine Stowe, Mary Stuart Masterson, Andie MacDowell and Drew Barrymore.
Brokedown Palace is a 1999 American drama film by Jonathan Kaplan.
The Accused is a 1988 Canadian-American drama film written by Tom Topor and directed by Jonathan Kaplan.
It stars Jodie Foster and Kelly McGillis.
The film is set in Washington state and was filmed in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada.
The film was loosely based on the 1983 gang rape of Cheryl Araujo in New Bedford, Massachusetts, and the resulting trial, which received national coverage.
This film was one of the first Hollywood films to portray rape graphically.
Brokedown Palace is a 1999 American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan, and starring Claire Danes, Kate Beckinsale, Bill Pullman and Lim Kay Tong.
It deals with two American friends imprisoned in Thailand for drug smuggling.
Because it presents a critical view of the Thai legal system, most scenes were filmed in the Philippines; however, some panoramas and views were filmed in Bangkok.
Its title is taken from a Grateful Dead song written by Jerry Garcia and Robert Hunter from their 1970 album "American Beauty".
Kathrin Romary Beckinsale (born 26 July 1973) is an English actress.
After some minor television roles, she made her film debut in "Much Ado About Nothing" (1993) while still a student at the University of Oxford.
She appeared in British costume dramas such as "Prince of Jutland" (1994), "Cold Comfort Farm" (1995), "Emma" (1996), and "The Golden Bowl" (2000), in addition to various stage and radio productions.

She began to seek film work in the United States in the late 1990s and, after appearing in small-scale dramas "The Last Days of Disco" (1998) and "Brokedown Palace" (1999), she had starring roles in the war drama "Pearl Harbor" and the romantic comedy "Serendipity".
She followed those with appearances in "The Aviator" (2004) and "Click" (2006).
Immediate Family is a 1989 drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan.
It stars Glenn Close and James Woods as a married childless couple who want a baby.
They decide to adopt from a pregnant teenage girl who later gets second thoughts.
Love Field is a 1992 American independent drama film written by Don Roos and directed by Jonathan Kaplan, starring Michelle Pfeiffer and Dennis Haysbert.
It was released on December 11, 1992 in the United States by Orion Pictures.
This film is an example of a representation of the assassination of John F. Kennedy in popular culture.
Over the Edge is a coming-of-age crime drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan and released in May 1979.
The film, based on a newspaper article, had a limited theatrical release but has since achieved cult film status.
It was Matt Dillon's film debut.
Unlawful Entry is a 1992 American psychological thriller film directed by Jonathan Kaplan.
The film stars Kurt Russell, Madeleine Stowe and Ray Liotta.
Reform School Girl is a 1994 American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: This American drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan starred an English actress who made her film debut in what?
hop1_docs: Jonathan Kaplan | Jonathan Kaplan (born November 25, 1947) is an American film producer and director.  His film "The Accused" (1988) earned actress Jodie Foster her first Oscar for Best Actress and was nominated for the Golden Bear at the 39th Berlin International Film Festival.  Kaplan received five Emmy nominations for his roles directing and producing "ER".

Immediate Family (film) | Immediate Family is a 1989 drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan.  It stars Glenn Close and James Woods as a married childless couple who want a baby.  They decide to adopt from a pregnant teenage girl who later gets second thoughts.

Project X (1987 film) | Project X is a 1987 American science fiction comedy-drama film produced by Walter F. Parkes and Lawrence Lasker, directed by Jonathan Kaplan, and starring Matthew Broderick and Helen Hunt.  The plot revolves around a USAF Airman (Broderick) and a graduate student (Hunt) who are assigned to care for chimpanzees used in a secret Air Force project.

Swing Shift (film) | Swing Shift is a 1984 American romantic drama war film directed by Jonathan Demme and produced by and starring Goldie Hawn with Kurt Russell.  It also starred Christine Lahti, Fred Ward and Ed Harris.  Lahti earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Supporting Actress for her tragic portrayal of heart-broken ex-singer and Hawn's character's close friend Hazel, losing to Peggy Ashcroft for "A Passage to India".  Singer Belinda Carlisle made a foray into the film, and Holly Hunter can be seen in one of her first movie roles.

Love Field (film) | Love Field is a 1992 American independent drama film written by Don Roos and directed by Jonathan Kaplan, starring Michelle Pfeiffer and Dennis Haysbert.  It was released on December 11, 1992 in the United States by Orion Pictures.  This film is an example of a representation of the assassination of John F. Kennedy in popular culture.

Sandra Bullock filmography | Sandra Bullock is an American actress who made her film debut with a minor role in the 1987 thriller "Hangmen".  She made her television debut in the television film "Bionic Showdown: The Six Million Dollar Man and the Bionic Woman" (1989) and played the lead role in the short-lived sitcom "Working Girl" (1990) before making her breakthrough starring in the action film "Speed" (1994).  She starred with Sylvester Stallone in "Demolition Man" (1994).  Bullock founded her own production company, Fortis Films, and starred in the romantic comedy "While You Were Sleeping" in 1995.  Her performance in the film earned her first nomination for the Golden Globe Award for Best Actress – Motion Picture Comedy or Musical.  The following year, Bullock starred with Matthew McConaughey in the film adaptation of John Grisham's novel "A Time to Kill" (1996).  In 1997, she reprised her "Speed" role in the sequel, "".  The following year, Bullock starred in the romantic comedy "Practical Magic", voiced Miriam in the animated biblical film "The Prince of Egypt" and also executive produced her first film, the romantic drama "Hope Floats".

Madison Wolfe | Madison Wolfe (born October 16, 2002) is an American actress.  She made her film debut in the adventure drama "On the Road" (2012) and her television debut in the HBO series "True Detective" (2014).  She starred in the horror film "The Conjuring 2" (2016).

- "Immediate Family" (1989) — passage: "Immediate Family is a 1989 drama film directed by Jonathan Kaplan. It stars Glenn Close and James Woods." (passage 2)
- "Project X" (1987) — passage: "Project X ... directed by Jonathan Kaplan, and starring Matthew Broderick and Helen Hunt." (passage 3)
- "Love Field" (1992) — passage: "Love Field is a 1992 ... film ... directed by Jonathan Kaplan, starring Michelle Pfeiffer and Dennis Haysbert." (passage 5)
- (Related fact) "Sandra Bullock ... made her film debut with a minor role in the 1987 thriller 'Hangmen'." (passage 6)

Missing / ambiguous: The passages do not identify any English actress who "starred" in a Jonathan Kaplan American drama, nor do they link an English actress to a specific Kaplan film; the required target fact ("which film the English actress made her film debut in") is missing. Bridge entity needed: the specific Kaplan-directed film that starred the English actress (and the actress's name).

Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
1. "Jonathan Kaplan filmography" or "List of films directed by Jonathan Kaplan" — inspect lede/Filmography and individual film cast lists.
2. Fetch the Wikipedia pages and "Cast" or lede/infobox sections for these Kaplan films (priority order): "The Accused (1988)", "Immediate Family (1989)", "Project X (1987)", "Love Field (1992)" — to locate any English actress in starring or principal cast.
3. If an English actress name is found, fetch that actress's Wikipedia page and inspect the lede/infobox and "Filmography" or "Career" sections for the phrase "made her film debut" (exact debut title and year).
4. Search keywords if needed: "Jonathan Kaplan starred English actress", "Jonathan Kaplan film starring English actress 'starred' 'Kaplan'".

hop2_docs: Jonathan Kaplan | Jonathan Kaplan (born November 25, 1947) is an American film producer and director.  His film "The Accused" (1988) earned actress Jodie Foster her first Oscar for Best Actress and was nominated for the Golden Bear at the 39th Berlin International Film Festival.  Kaplan received five Emmy nominations for his roles directing and producing "ER".

Božidara Turzonovová filmography | The filmography of Bulgarian-born Slovak actress Božidara Turzonovová chronicles her work through the artist's fifty years as a film, television and stage actress.  She entered film industry in 1961, and made her official cinematic debut in the Vladislav Pavlovič's production of "Most na tú stranu".  Overall, she appeared in one-hundred-sixty-eight titles to date, of which thirty-five are feature, and one-hundred-thirty-five television films or series.  While on stage, Turzonovová was cast in one-hundred-seven plays or musicals, eight of which have been also televised.
Wendy Richard filmography | The Wendy Richard filmography lists the film and TV appearances of English actress Wendy Richard.
Joan Sims filmography | The Joan Sims filmography lists the film and television appearances of English actress Joan Sims (1930–2001).

Jonathan Kaplan (rugby union) | Jonathan Isaac Kaplan (born 7 November 1966 in Durban, South Africa), is a former international rugby union referee who used to hold the record for the most international matches as referee.

Ruby Lin filmography | This is the filmography of the Taiwanese actress-turned-producer Ruby Lin.  After playing in various series and films, her production debut turned out to be a hit with viewers.  Chinese media considered Lin served as a shining example for star-turned-producer.

Spice Girls filmography | English pop girl group the Spice Girls have starred in one feature film, as well as several television specials, documentaries and commercials.  They made their film debut in 1997, starring in their feature film "Spice World".  The film was a commercial success, but was widely panned by critics, earning the group the Golden Raspberry Award for Worst Actress.
